title: Orders (comic story)

{{Quote|We've got no homes, no mothers to raise us, no fathers to guide us, but we were thrown into a war and trained to die for a Republic we'd never even seen. We've got nothing but each other and our orders.|Sarge|<ref name="Orders">{{CWACite|4|Orders (comic story)}}</ref>}}
""'''Orders (comic)'''"" was a [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] story from ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures Volume 4]]'', the fourth volume in the [[Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures (graphic novels)|''Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures'' graphic novels]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series. It primarily focused on the lives of four [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Clone commando/Legends|commandos]] from [[Aiwha Squad]], their interaction with a [[Evan|young refugee]] from the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], and their execution of [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Traavis]] in accordance with [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]].

==Plot summary==
In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Battle of Garqi (Clone Wars)|battled]] the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Agriworld/Legends|agricultural]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Garqi/Legends|Garqi]]. After the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] accidentally bombed civilian territory in an attempt to destroy the Separatists' [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]], the [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commandos]] of [[Aiwha Squad]] were sent in to search for survivors. They found a young [[Gender/Legends|boy]] named [[Evan]], whose parents had been [[Death/Legends|killed]], in the bombed out remains of a [[Farm/Legends|farm]] house. Per their orders, Aiwha [[Squad/Legends|Squad]] escorted the child toward the nearby [[Garqi refugee camp|refugee camp]], but while en route, Evan attempted to escape from the [[Cloning/Legends|clones]], only to encounter an [[OG-9 homing spider droid/Legends|OG-9 homing spider droid]]. Aiwha Squad's [[Clone Sergeant/Legends|sergeant]], [[Sarge (clone commando)|RC-1013]], drew the [[spider droid]]'s attention to himself to protect the boy, and once he had drawn the [[Droid/Legends|droid's]] fire Aiwha Squad struck, [[Blaster/Legends|attacking]] and destroying the droid.

As the [[Star/Legends|sun]] set over the horizon, Aiwha Squad decided to make camp for the night. Leisurely sitting around a fire without helmets and assorted pieces of [[Armor/Legends|armor]], Evan inquired as to why the four [[Commando/Legends|commandos]] all looked the same. [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] RC-1013—introducing himself as "Sarge"—explained that they were clones, and introduced the other members of his squad: [[Zag]], [[Tyto]], and [[Di'kut]]. Evan explained his own predicament, including how he'd become an orphan during the battle, and Sarge expressed his sympathies before explaining the nature of their lives in the clone [[Army/Legends|army]] and the importance of obeying orders in war. The following morning, Aiwha Squad arrives at the refugee camp with Evan and deliver him into the custody of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and [[Jedi General/Legends|General]], [[Traavis]]. Traavis commended the squad on their efforts and relayed news of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]]' death while Sarge took a moment to offer some words of advice to Evan before parting. However, mere moments later, Sarge received the transmission from [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] containing an order to carry out [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]]. Obediently, Sarge ordered his men to comply and shot down General Traavis as a traitor to the Republic, right before Evan's eyes. Seeing the child crying and confused, Sarge explained the meaning behind Order 66—that the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] were traitors to the Republic who were to be killed—and reiterated his belief in obeying the orders given to him.

==Continuity==
Although not stated in the comic itself, author [[Ryan Kaufman (real person)|Ryan Kaufman]] explains on the [[Jedi Council Forums]] that "Orders" takes place on the planet Garqi.<ref name="JCF">{{JCFCite|url=posts/22004315|board=Literature|thread=Clone Wars Adventures Volume 4 Discussion|user=[[Ryan Kaufman (real person)|Ryan_Kaufman]]|date=2005-10-19|time=11:13 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801145615/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/clone-wars-adventures-volume-4-discussion.21403232/page-2}}</ref> The next story in ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures Volume 4]]'', "''[[Descent (comic story)|Descent]]''", references the battle occurring in this story.
